title: The Big Picture: Slower start to 2025 for global trade in iron ore
section_path: The Big Picture: Slower start to 2025 for global trade in iron ore
section_path_text: The Big Picture: Slower start to 2025 for global trade in iron ore
summary:
Main
Judging by customs data gathered by Braemar, more than 120m tonnes of iron ore exports were added to annual seaborne trade in 2023 and 2024 combined-largely fuelled by China's import appetite. However, in the first four months of 2025 global seaborne iron ore trade reversed the recent trend, falling behind the year-ago pace. Several supply-side constraints have been apparent in the 90%-Capesize iron ore trade this year.
